# AR-recall probe results — 2026-07-03

Run: `railway run uv run python -m scripts.knowledge_ar_recall_probe` (sage-api/production corpus, read-only). 28 queries (14 info-needs × {khaleeji, msa}).

## Numbers (rewrite OFF vs ON — identical)

| bucket | n | recall@5 OFF | recall@5 ON | MRR OFF | MRR ON |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| overall | 28 | 1.000 | 1.000 | 0.846 | 0.846 |
| msa/baseline | 14 | 1.000 | 1.000 | 0.893 | 0.893 |
| khaleeji/orthographic | 5 | 1.000 | 1.000 | 0.900 | 0.900 |
| khaleeji/lexical | 9 | 1.000 | 1.000 | 0.744 | 0.744 |

## Interpretation

- **Deploy Gate 1 (no MSA regression): PASS.** OFF and ON are byte-identical on `msa/baseline` — the query-side-only normalization did NOT desync standard MSA queries from the un-normalized corpus on this set. The whole-branch review's §Important-1 risk did not materialize here.
- **No measurable lift either.** recall@5 is at CEILING (1.0) across all buckets: the current AR corpus is small (~21 articles) and `top_k=5` retrieves the gold chunk regardless of normalization. Rank-sensitive MRR is also unchanged, so orthographic normalization does not alter retrieval on this set — BGE-M3 vector similarity is already robust to these variants at this scale.
- **Measurement ceiling caveat.** This probe confirms *no-regression* but cannot demonstrate *lift* at the current corpus size. A more sensitive setup (recall@1, or a larger corpus where recall@5 is not saturated) is needed to detect real differences. This asset + score capture feed Item #2 (abstain-threshold calibration).
- **Interpretive caveat (native reviewer):** the 5 `khaleeji/orthographic` rows are constructed spelling-variant pairs, not authored Gulf triggers (no authored trigger is orthographic-only). They measure casual Arabic spelling variation, not genuine Gulf dialect divergence, which is lexical (the 9 `khaleeji/lexical` rows) and out of the v1 orthographic rewriter's reach.

## Value delivered by the change (independent of probe lift)
Spec-compliance (rewriter no longer dead code, both paths), audit trace (`query_raw`/`query_searched`), Azure-swap-safe base-layer placement, and the reusable TD5-forward measurement asset.
